QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_16

question.text: How often is the NAV of an open-ended mutual fund calculated?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The NAV of an open-ended mutual fund is calculated at the end of every business day. It reflects the current market value of all the securities held by the fund on that day, minus expenses, divided by total units outstanding. **The Trap:** NAV is not a weekly, monthly, or annual figure — it changes every business day because the market value of the underlying securities changes daily. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under NAV computation. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When clients ask "what is the value of my investment today?", check the latest NAV. It is published on the AMC website and AMFI website (amfiindia.com) every evening for all schemes.
